title: ruscus streptophyllus
intro: Ruscus streptophyllus, also known as Butcher's Broom, is a perennial plant in the Asparagaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region, and is commonly used in herbal medicine. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating circulatory issues and inflammation, due to its high content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Butcher's Broom include improved blood circulation, reduced swelling, and relief from symptoms of varicose veins and hemorrhoids. Herbal preparations of Ruscus streptophyllus typically include dried root and rhizome extracts, which can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures for medicinal use.
